title: AWS CloudTrail integration goes GA
summary: Huntress's AWS CloudTrail log-source integration moved from early access to General Availability with an improved onboarding workflow.
why_it_matters: Adds AWS account-level activity into the same MDR pipeline that watches Windows endpoints — same analyst team, same triage SLA, broader coverage.

title: Linux agent gains Syslog collection
summary: The Huntress Linux agent now ships native Syslog collection, expanding coverage beyond Windows endpoints.
why_it_matters: Linux servers (web, app, file) can now contribute to the same threat-hunting telemetry as Windows endpoints — useful for hybrid fleets.

title: Phishing & Security Awareness reports go GA
summary: Standard phishing and learning reports moved into General Availability, with further enhancements planned through 2026.
why_it_matters: Security Awareness Training results land in the same console as MDR and ITDR — single pane of glass for human-risk metrics.

title: Huntress stops VMware ESXi zero-day attack chain
summary: Huntress observed and stopped a multi-stage VMware ESXi attack that used VSOCK for backdoor communication to bypass traditional network monitoring. The activity was caught before it could progress to the ransomware deployment stage.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates active threat-hunting catching sophisticated VM-escape tradecraft that signature-based tools miss — the kind of detection that justifies an MDR over plain antivirus.
